title: Vendor in latest container/storage for devicemapper support
body:
container/storage now supports devicemapper options that allow you to
configure it.

Signed-off-by: Daniel J Walsh <dwalsh@redhat.com>

comments.body:
Change LGTM, but issues in tests here too:

```
# time="2018-05-19T10:30:20Z" level=debug msg="GET https://registry.access.redhat.com/v2/alpine/manifests/latest"
# time="2018-05-19T10:30:21Z" level=debug msg="error pulling image \"registry.access.redhat.com/alpine\": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/alpine:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/alpine: unknown: Not Found"
# time="2018-05-19T10:30:21Z" level=debug msg="error pulling and reading image \"registry.access.redhat.com/alpine\": Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/alpine:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/alpine: unknown: Not Found"
# time="2018-05-19T10:30:21Z" level=error msg="Error determining manifest MIME type for docker://registry.access.redhat.com/alpine:latest: Error reading manifest latest in registry.access.redhat.com/alpine: unknown: Not Found"
ok 86 from cpuset-mems test

```

@rhatdan To take `devicemapper` out of the list of drivers that we attempt to use when none is specified and there isn't evidence that one was previously used for that particular storage root, removing it from the `priority` list in `drivers/driver_linux.go` should work.
@nalind I am attempting to fix this with this change. https://github.com/containers/storage/pull/178

@nalind @TomSweeneyRedHat @umohnani8 PTAL
I think this is ready to merge.
LGTM and happy green test buttons.

:pushpin: Commit a1f6e87 has been approved by `umohnani8`

<!-- @rh-atomic-bot r=umohnani8 a1f6e872ba43ea1d6ea29e90c2e6b59f0b2460fe -->
:zap: Test exempted: pull fully rebased and already tested.
